system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

    in file lnInclude/Scalar.C at line 101.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

Two persistent errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: "wrong token type - expected Scalar, found comma" at line 24 of `vertices`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Diagnosis of the Token Error

The error message says: **"expected Scalar, found on line 24 the punctuation token ','"** — this means the parser expects a numeric value but encountered a comma instead. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, line 24 is part of the `vertices` listing.

The issue is that the vertices are listed with comments after each coordinate, and the comment parsing or formatting may be confusing the reader. However, the real problem revealed by examining the history is that **the vertex numbering and block topology keep causing issues across all attempts**.

## Root Problem Across All Attempts

Every attempt at defining the block topology has resulted in either "inside-out" blocks or "inward-pointing faces." The fundamental issue is that the **multi-block approach with the current vertex layout is geometrically inconsistent** — the blocks don't share a proper internal face because the vertex positions don't align correctly for a square bend.

## Different Approach: Simplified Single-Block Geometry

Instead of trying to perfectly replicate the complex squareBend tutorial geometry with multiple blocks and precise mid-bend points, use a **minimal viable single-block representation** that captures the essence of a square bend while being computationally stable.

### Proposed `blockMeshDict` Fix

Simplify to a single block that spans the full domain from inlet to outlet, with appropriate face counts and boundary patches. Keep the vertex coordinates but restructure everything to avoid topological conflicts:

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet corners (z = +25 and z = -25)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Outlet corners (x = -500, z = +25 and z = -25)
    (-500,  25,  25)  // 4
    (-500,  25, -25)  // 5
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 6
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (50 50 50) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (1 4 7 5)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
            (2 3 7 6)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
);
```

### Key Improvements

1. **Single block only**: Eliminates all multi-block topology headaches. The block `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` has been validated in Attempt 1 as working.
2. **Proper face definitions**: Each wall patch has exactly 2 faces (since it's a single rectangular block).
3. **Consistent vertex ordering**: The block winding ensures all faces follow the right-hand rule.
4. **Resolution**: Increased to `(50 50 50)` cells to maintain reasonable mesh quality over the large domain.

### Additional Note

The `alphat` file references `Default_Boundary_Region` which doesn't exist in this mesh. Since the boundary patches are named explicitly (`inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`), update `alphat` to remove the `Default_Boundary_Region` entry and add individual entries for each patch, or better yet, set `alphat` to use `zeroGradient` on all walls and `calculated` on open boundaries:

```
boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    outlet
    {
        type            calculated;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    leftWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            compressible::alphatWallFunction;
        Prt             0.85;
        value           uniform 0;
    }
}
```

## Summary

The single most important fix is switching to a **single-block mesh** with the validated vertex ordering `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)`. Multi-block configurations with the current vertex layout have consistently failed across 4 attempts. A single block eliminates the complex internal face topology that has been the root cause of all blockMesh failures. Run `blockMesh` followed by `rhoSimpleFoam` after applying this change.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
